title: [InstCombine] Fold `icmp spred (mul nsw X, Z), (mul nsw Y, Z)` into `icmp spred X, Y`

tests.source_program:
define i1 @icmp_mul_nuw_ult_i32(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %mul1 = mul nuw i32 %x, 7
  %mul2 = mul nuw i32 %y, 7
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %mul1, %mul2
  ret i1 %cmp
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on signed comparisons with nsw flags; this tests unsigned comparisons with nuw flags which are handled by the same patch but not emphasized in the summary text

alive2.src:
define i1 @icmp_mul_nuw_ult_i32(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %mul1 = mul nuw i32 %x, 7
  %mul2 = mul nuw i32 %y, 7
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %mul1, %mul2
  ret i1 %cmp
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i1 @icmp_mul_nuw_ult_i32(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %x, %y
  ret i1 %cmp
}

----------------------------------------

tests.source_program:
define i1 @icmp_mul_nsw_eq_i64_odd_multiplier(i64 %x, i64 %y) {
entry:
  %mul1 = mul nsw i64 %x, 7
  %mul2 = mul nsw i64 %y, 7
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %mul1, %mul2
  ret i1 %cmp
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary emphasizes the extension to signed relational comparisons; equality comparisons with odd multiplier (trailing zeros = 0) are handled by the patch but not highlighted in the summary

alive2.src:

tests.source_program:
define i1 @icmp_mul_nsw_sge_neg_constant(i8 %x, i8 %y) {
entry:
  %mul1 = mul nsw i8 %x, -5
  %mul2 = mul nsw i8 %y, -5
  %cmp = icmp sge i8 %mul1, %mul2
  ret i1 %cmp
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions predicate swapping for negative Z but existing known tests only show slt with negative; sge with negative constant tests the swapped predicate (sge->sle) which is not explicitly demonstrated

alive2.src:
define i1 @icmp_mul_nsw_sge_neg_constant(i8 %x, i8 %y) {
entry:
  %mul1 = mul nsw i8 %x, -5
  %mul2 = mul nsw i8 %y, -5
  %cmp = icmp sge i8 %mul1, %mul2
  ret i1 %cmp
}
